pr.title: Only use code bytes for `wiki_tu` percentages
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Updated the wiki translation-unit reporting script so displayed completion percentages are calculated from matched code bytes divided by total code bytes, instead of reusing `fuzzy_match_percent`. This makes `wiki_tu` percentages align with the code-byte totals already shown in the table.
what_changed: In `tools/wiki_tu.py`, introduced local `matched_code` and `total_code` float values from `unit["matched_code"]` and `unit["total_code"]`.
Computed `code_percent` as `(matched_code / total_code if total_code else 0) * 100`, explicitly guarding against division by zero.
Changed the displayed percent field from `unit['fuzzy_match_percent']` to the newly computed code-byte percentage, still formatted with `humanfriendly.round_number`.
Kept the existing friendly byte-size display for `matched_code` and `total_code` unchanged.
smart_moves: Made the percentage numerator and denominator match the table columns being displayed, avoiding a mismatch between byte counts and fuzzy-match-derived percentages.
Used a zero-total guard before division, which keeps the wiki-generation tool robust for empty or malformed translation-unit records.
Kept formatting behavior stable by continuing to use `humanfriendly.round_number`, minimizing presentation churn while fixing the metric source.
decomp_lessons: Progress-reporting tools should compute percentages from the same metric family shown to users; if the table displays code bytes, the percentage should be based on code bytes too.
Do not assume fuzzy match percentages are interchangeable with matched-code-byte percentages; they may represent different progress semantics.
Small tooling fixes can matter for decomp coordination because inaccurate wiki progress metrics can mislead prioritization and ownership decisions.
When calculating generated-report metrics, handle zero totals explicitly so edge-case translation units do not break automation.
naming_conventions: `matched_code` and `total_code` are used as metric keys for byte-based code matching totals.
`code_percent` is a clear local name for the derived matched-code-over-total-code percentage.
The script keeps presentation helper naming such as `friendly_size`, `file_link`, `matched`, `total`, and `percent`.
follow_up_queries: Find other tooling that displays `fuzzy_match_percent` next to code-byte totals and verify the metric source is intentional.
Audit generated wiki progress tables for consistency between displayed byte counts and percentage calculations.
Check whether `total_code == 0` translation units should be omitted, shown as 0%, or handled with a special marker.
